data.title: Siemens secures $390M contract for NYC's Fulton-Liberty subway lines
data.description:
Siemens (SIEGY) said Monday it was awarded a contract valued at nearly $390M in collaboration with L.K. Comstock, an electrical contractor that specializes in rail transit systems, by the Metropolitan Transportation Authority to modernize New York City's Fulton-Liberty subway lines.

Siemens Mobility (SIEGY) said it will modernize 23 stations, replace outdated track circuits, and introduce 5G-based radio communications to power real-time, optimized train control that will make it safe for trains to run as close as 90 seconds apart, replacing century-old infrastructure in Brooklyn and Queens.

The scope of work includes the installation and replacement of 24 new special work portions, 94 switch machines, and four new train control rooms; the company said the upgrades require more than 1,200 km of cable to be installed by L.K. Comstock throughout the tunnels and within the newly built facilities.

Siemens (SIEGY) said it will also take on a 25-year maintenance contract with the option for two additional five-year extensions once the system modernization is complete.

The Fulton Line travels through central Brooklyn and connects to the Liberty Line, which begins at Euclid Avenue Station and concludes in Ozone Park, Queens.

data.title: Repay stock jumps 11% after preliminary Q1 adjusted EBITDA tops consensus
data.description:
Repay Holdings (RPAY) stock surged 11% to $4.08 in late morning trading after the payment technology company issued preliminary Q1 results and raised its guidance for 2026 adjusted EBITDA. The company completed a buyout of a strategic distribution partner in Q1, resulting in a one-time cash payment that had a positive impact on its adjusted EBITDA, it said.

The stock's advance followed a jump earlier this month when its largest shareholder, Forager Capital Management, proposed to acquire Repay (RPAY) for $4.80 per share.

Preliminary Q1 revenue of $80.5M-$81.0M compares with the average analyst estimate of $80.9M. Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be $33.8M-$34.3M, exceeding the $32.8M consensus estimate. That brings its Q1 adjusted EBITDA margin to ~42% vs. 42% in Q4 2025.

Repay (RPAY) now expects full-year adjusted EBITDA of $141M-$146M, up from its initial outlook of $136.5M-$141.5M. The revised range puts the guidance above the consensus of $137.9M. The company still expects 2026 revenue of $340M-$346M and free cash flow conversion rate of 45%.

data.title: Starbucks Q2 2026 earnings preview: What is brewing amid turnaround?
data.description:
Starbucks (SBUX) is scheduled to announce Q2 earnings results on Tuesday, April 28th, after market close.

The coffeehouse chain has been undergoing an overhaul, with CEO Brian Niccol highlighting in the recent annual general meeting that the company is ahead of schedule in its turnaround and that “the shine is back on Starbucks.”

More recently, Jeffries upgraded the stock to Hold, catalyzed by the franchising of its China business. 

“We think there is somewhat low visibility into global growth accelerating meaningfully in out years, but the bar/expectations are set low enough,” said Jefferies’ Andy Barish.

SA contributor SMR Finance expects the Q2 earnings to “mark an inflection point, with 3–4% comparable sales growth and the first YoY operating margin improvement after eight quarters of contraction.”

“Starbucks has outperformed YTD, reflecting growing market optimism about the Niccol-led turnaround and operational improvements,” according to the analysis.

Early in the month, contributor Yi Hu, CFA, was more bearish, estimating a margin squeeze despite modest top-line recovery amid macro headwinds and intensifying competition.

The consensus EPS Estimate is $0.44 (+7.3% Y/Y), and the consensus revenue estimate is $9.23B (+5.4% Y/Y).

Over the last 2 years, SBUX has beaten EPS estimates 0% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 38% of the time.

Over the last 3 months, EPS estimates have seen 9 upward revisions and 2 downward. Revenue estimates have seen 9 upward revisions and 1 downward.

data.title: AI, chip stocks fall ahead of Big Tech earnings, Iran war developments
data.description:
Chip and AI-related stocks were in the red on Monday as investors awaited Big Tech earnings, the upcoming Federal Reserve decision, and developments in the U.S.-Iran war.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) dipped around 0.25%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) declined about 0.12%. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) also fell nearly 0.24%.

Tech giants Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL), Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), and Meta Platforms (META) are slated to report results on Wednesday, while Apple (AAPL) reports on Thursday.

Iran has offered to end its stronghold on the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for the U.S. lifting its blockade on the country and an end to the war, The Associated Press reported on Monday, citing two regional officials. Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi was in Russia on Monday for a meeting with President Vladimir Putin as part of a trip that included two stops in Pakistan, where leaders are trying to restart stalled talks between Iran and the U.S.

Reportedly, at least seven ships — mainly dry bulk vessels — have crossed the Strait of Hormuz in ‌the past 24 hours, as the vital waterway remains effectively shut to international shipping. Separately, Adrian Bisserie, CEO of Ducat Maritime noted that the disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz are creating inflationary pressures equivalent to roughly half of what was seen during the COVID-19 pandemic.

Brent Futures (CO1:COM) jumped about 3.17%, while Crude Oil Futures (CL1:COM) climbed nearly 2.97% on Monday.

Shares of AI chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) climbed about 1%, while Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) fell nearly 4%. Broadcom (AVGO) declined around 2%, while Qualcomm (QCOM) was also in the red.

Several other AI and networking-related stocks were in the red. Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) plummeted about 10%, while Coherent (COHR) and Lumentum (LITE) each tumbled nearly 5%. Corning (GLW) and Arista Networks (ANET) each slumped about 4%, while Ciena (CIEN) fell around 3%. Cisco (CSCO) dipped about 1%, while Celestica (CLS) was also in the red.

Arm (ARM) slumped about 9%, while Marvell Technology (MRVL) tumbled nearly 5%. Lattice Semiconductor (LSCC) and GlobalFoundries (GFS) each fell nearly 3%, while Texas Instruments (TXN) and Analog Devices (ADI) declined about 2%. Meanwhile, some stocks seemed to buck the trend. Micron Technology (MU) jumped around 5%, while Intel (INTC) rose about 1%. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) was also in the green.

Chip equipment makers: Applied Materials (AMAT) fell about 3%, while ASML (ASML), KLA (KLAC), and Lam Research (LRCX) each declined around 2%.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: POET Technologies crashes as it loses deal with Marvell
data.description:
POET Technologies (POET) shares crashed nearly 50% on Monday after the company disclosed it had lost a contract with Celestial AI, which has been acquired by Marvell Technology (MRVL).

“As the basis for the cancellation, Marvell indicated that the Company had made disclosures of information related to the Purchase Order and shipping information in contravention of its confidentiality obligations,” POET said in a statement.

POET added that it remains focused on “executing its strategic priorities and advancing product development within the AI and optical networking markets to meet increasing demand.”

Marvell shares fell 5.5% in late morning trading.

The sharp decline comes just days after POET shares surged amid speculation that tied the company to Marvell Technology and Nvidia's (NVDA) hardware ecosystem. CFO Thomas Mika confirmed the Marvell Technology connection in an interview with Stocktwits. 

“We've got a purchase order from them [Marvell] that we're gonna ship against and we intend to continue that relationship and build it over time,” Mika said, noting the order was linked to Celestial AI.

“When you’re dealing with large companies who have committed to designing modules using your components, sometimes you hear from them, and sometimes you don’t,” Mika added. “We expect to hear back from at least one of those.”

Marvell completed its $3.25B acquisition of Celestial AI in February.

data.title: Only 11 mega-caps stand out in SA Quant Ratings as markets hover near highs
data.description:
Wall Street’s major indices opened the week near record highs, reinforcing underlying strength in equities. Yet much of the market’s momentum is concentrated in a select group of names, according to Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating system.

Among U.S. mega-cap stocks, just 11 currently carry a “Buy” or “Strong Buy” rating under the model.

The narrow leadership underscores a more selective market backdrop, where investors are prioritizing companies with durable earnings and strong fundamentals. Seeking Alpha’s Quant framework ranks stocks using a data-driven approach that incorporates valuation, growth, profitability, and momentum—helping identify companies with sustained outperformance potential. 

Below are the 11 mega-cap stocks, ranked by their Quant ratings: 

  1. Micron Technology (MU), Quant Rating of 4.99. 
  2. Intel Corporation (INTC), Quant Rating of 4.99.
  3.  Exxon Mobil Corporation (XOM), Quant Rating of 4.95.
  4. Chevron Corporation (CVX), Quant Rating of 4.94. 
  5. Texas Instruments (TXN), Quant Rating of 4.93. 
  6. Bank of America (BAC), Quant Rating of 4.91.
  7. Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), Quant Rating of 4.90.
  8. PepsiCo (PEP), Quant Rating of 4.64. 
  9. Philip Morris International (PM), Quant Rating of 4.45. 
  10. RTX Corporation (RTX), Quant Rating of 4.42. 
  11. AbbVie (ABBV), Quant Rating of 4.25.

S&P 500 Funds: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (FXAIX), (VFIAX), (VFFSX), and (SWPPX).

data.title: UL Solutions added as a new long idea at Hedgeye
data.description:
UL Solutions was added as a new long idea at Hedgeye.

"ULS has performed strongly since its pre-IPO recommendation two years ago and remains a high-quality, long-term compounder," Hedgeye analyst Jay Van Sciver wrote in a note on Sunday. "Like other durable franchises that have transitioned to for-profit models, it still has meaningful runway to improve capital allocation through M&A, revenue growth, and margin expansion."

"Although the strategy briefly shifted toward more cyclical names heading into 2026, that view has reversed," Hedgeye's Van Sciver added. "The current macro backdrop and ULS’s ongoing consolidation and self-improvement story support re-engaging with the stock. The company remains a top long-term idea, with continued growth opportunities across industrial, consumer, energy, and AI/technology end markets, underpinned by strong brand equity and customer relationships."

UL Solutions (ULS) is expected to report Q1 results next Tuesday.  Shares of UL Solutions fell 1.7% on Monday.

data.title: Microsoft falls as OpenAI partnership evolves, says it will no longer pay revenue share
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) shares fell fractionally in early trading on Monday as the tech giant and OpenAI (OPENAI) said their partnership has continued to evolve, and OpenAI's license will become non-exclusive. 

“Today, we are announcing an amended agreement to simplify our partnership and the way we work together, grounded in flexibility, certainty and a focus on delivering the benefits of AI broadly,” Microsoft wrote in a statement on its website. “The greater predictability in the amended agreement strengthens our joint ability to build and operate AI platforms at scale while providing both companies the flexibility to pursue new opportunities.”

As part of the altered agreement, Microsoft will remain OpenAI's primary cloud partner, and OpenAI products will ship on Azure first. However, there is now a tweak that says if Microsoft “cannot and chooses not to support the necessary capabilities,” OpenAI can go elsewhere. 

Julian Lin, Investing Group Leader for Best Of Breed Growth Stocks, said the deal is actually a “net positive” for Microsoft, despite the share price reaction.

“While it does indeed explicitly allow OpenAI to work with other cloud providers, that isn't necessarily anything new and was inevitable,” Lin said. “The most important takeaway is that MSFT has locked in its 20% revenue share from OpenAI regardless of whether or not the company reaches AGI through 2030. This development appears to indicate that OpenAI is far further along than expected, with MSFT expected to benefit from both the revenue share as well as the cloud commitments over the long term.”

In addition, OpenAI can bring all of its products to customers across any cloud provider, such as Amazon (AMZN) Web Services. Microsoft will also continue to have a license to use OpenAI's intellectual property for its models and products through 2032, but the license will now be non-exclusive. 

Amazon shares gave up earlier gains and fell 0.8% in late morning trading following the news.

As part of the amended deal, Microsoft will no longer pay a revenue share to OpenAI, though Microsoft will receive revenue share payments from OpenAI through 2030, regardless of its technological progress. 

Microsoft is still a major shareholder of OpenAI, the Windows maker added.

Seeking Alpha analyst Julia Ostian said the fact that an estimated 45% of Microsoft's AI backlog comes from OpenAI is a “major risk” for the Satya Nadella-led firm. However, it's likely any risks are already priced into Microsoft shares. 

“The market didn’t fully believe OpenAI would be able to pay for the previously agreed capacity, and for this reason, I don’t see this piece of news as majorly negative,” Ostian said via email.

Microsoft and OpenAI last amended their landmark agreement late in 2025, with a tweak in October to support its changing corporate structure.

(This story has been updated with additional analyst commentary.)

data.title: Tax refunds (temporarily) cushion consumers from gas price shock - Pantheon Macro
data.description:
Strong individual tax refunds are helping U.S. consumers weather the surge in gasoline (UNG) prices, but the support is expected to fade quickly as the normal filing season ends, according to a report from Pantheon Macroeconomics published Monday.

March tax refunds totaled $96B, up $14B from March 2025, thanks to tax cuts in the Trump administration's One Big Beautiful Bill Act, Pantheon economists Samuel Tombs and Oliver Allen wrote. That windfall has more than offset the hit from higher gas (UNG) prices, which have risen sharply amid the ongoing conflict in the Middle East.

“The resulting near-1% lift to incomes is dominating the gas price shock for now,” the note said.

Retail gasoline sales rose $9B year-over-year in March but are projected to climb about $15B in April. The economists expect tax refunds will continue providing support through April, with daily data through April 22 pointing to refunds of just over $100B for the month, up roughly $20B from the prior year.

However, because the normal tax filing deadline passed on April 15, refund flows will slow sharply in May.

Pantheon projects consumer spending was relatively strong in March, rising 0.3%, driven by healthy goods spending including a jump in auto sales. Solid gains in furniture and electronics suggest households used refunds to fund big-ticket purchases.

Pantheon expects consumer spending in the second quarter will be little changed from the first quarter as the refund support fades while gas prices remain elevated.

data.title: Greenland Energy surges after signing Halliburton deal for Jameson drilling campaign
data.description:
Greenland Energy (GLND) pulls back after surging as much as 27% in early trading Monday after signing an agreement with Halliburton (HAL) for integrated consulting services and logistical management related to well and drilling services for its onshore campaign in the Jameson Basin.

Greenland Energy (GLND) said its agreement with Halliburton (HAL) forms a key component of its integrated Arctic operations strategy, ensuring best‑in‑class rig performance, logistics, and subsurface technology for the first onshore exploration well in the Jameson Basin.

Following more than a year of logistical planning and site preparation, Greenland Energy (GLND) said it expects to drill its first two wells in 2026.

The Jameson Land Basin spans ~2M acres and includes multiple identified targets supported by seismic data.
